video:video.video:title: Paris voters head to polls to elect new mayor
video:video.video:description: Parisian voters will head to the polls Sunday to elect their mayor in a closely watched race that could reshape politics in the French capital.

video:video.video:title: Coco Jones reveals what Ciara taught her
video:video.video:description: At an Essence Black Women in Hollywood event in Los Angeles, Coco Jones revealed that her best mentor has been Ciara.

video:video.video:title: A record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
video:video.video:description: A record 280 political parties and counting had registered by Thursday’s deadline to participate in Haiti’s first general election in a decade, although not all will make the cut.

video:video.video:title: 2 Ohio pastors emerge as faithful allies for Haitian migrants during Trump's crackdown
video:video.video:description: One grew up in rural Haiti amid the poverty and violence. The other grew up in Michigan as a self-described “blue-collar farm kid."

video:video.video:title: Snow geese take off for the Arctic in mesmerizing sunrise display
video:video.video:description: Thousands of snow geese touch down at a Pennsylvania reservoir each spring, turning sunrise into a loud, swirling liftoff that draws big crowds.

video:video.video:title: How to read the AP election maps
video:video.video:description: It's the 2026 midterm season, with key state races drawing national attention as voters weigh the balance of power in Washington.

video:video.video:title: Air raid sirens sound in Jerusalem as missile strikes Holon about 40 miles away
video:video.video:description: Air alert sirens sounded in Jerusalem again and a missile hit an open area on the Tel Aviv satellite town of Holon on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy offers countries assistance to combat Iranian drones he says Russia uses against Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Friday that Ukraine has received requests from six countries for drone combat assistance.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters and rescuers dispatched to site of Iranian missile hit in Central Israel
video:video.video:description: Firefighters and rescuers were dispatched to the location of an Iranian missile hit in an open area in Central Israel on Friday. (AP video by Sam Mednick)

video:video.video:title: Iranian sailors’ bodies arrive at Mattala Airport in Sri Lanka ahead of repatriation
video:video.video:description: Sri Lanka's foreign ministry said Friday that the remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in a U.S. submarine attack last week had been handed over to Iranian representatives to be repatriated.

video:video.video:title: Fire Engulfs Boat off Miami Beach
video:video.video:description: A 40 foot boat was on fire in Miami Beach, Florida on Thursday. There were no reported injuries, with Miami Beach Fire Department safely removing all occupants from the vessel.

video:video.video:title: 'Don't need to worry about it': Hegseth says Iran 'exercising sheer desperation' in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Iran is “exercising sheer desperation” in the Strait of Hormuz, which the war has effectively closed, blocking a significant portion of the world’s oil.

video:video.video:title: Explosion rocks Tehran during government-organized rally, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: A large explosion rocked an area of Iran’s capital where thousands were gathered Friday for an annual state-organized rally to support the Palestinians and call for Israel’s demise.

video:video.video:title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of targeting civilians after fatal airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of targeting civilians after fatal airstrikes

video:video.video:title: 5 indelible AP photos from Hollywood's awards season
video:video.video:description: As a staff photojournalist for The Associated Press, Chris Pizzello covers hundreds of entertainment events a year.

video:video.video:title: US Attorney Pirro to appeal judge's ruling against Fed subpoenas
video:video.video:description: Judge James Boasberg said that a “mountain of evidence suggests" that the purpose of the subpoenas was simply to pressure the Fed to cut its key interest rate, as President Donald Trump has repeatedly demanded.

video:video.video:title: At least 8 people killed in Israeli strike in Sidon, Lebanon
video:video.video:description: At least eight people have been killed in an Israeli strike in the southern coastal city of Sidon on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Family members of a man suspected of attacking a Michigan synagogue were killed in an Israeli airstrike last week, AP source says
video:video.video:description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali lost two of his brothers, a niece and a nephew during an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon, an official said.

video:video.video:title: Oregon Army National Guard hoists injured logger to safety in Mount Hood National Forest
video:video.video:description: An Oregon Army National Guard helicopter safely hoisted an injured logger from a remote national forest on Thursday after steep, rugged terrain prevented other means of rescue, fire department officials said.

video:video.video:title: All but 2 of Austria's 96 glaciers have retreated over last 2 years, experts say
video:video.video:description: All but two of Austria’s 96 glaciers have retreated over the last two years, monitors in the Alpine country reported Friday, saying the “dramatic development” highlights the impact of climate change.

video:video.video:title: Brazil's telenovela industry is the secret agent behind powerful films at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Hollywood actors might rule the silver screen — as Sunday’s Academy Awards is poised to prove — but Brazil’s path to stardom often starts under the bright lights of a TV studio rather than a sprawling movie set.

video:video.video:title: Injured logger rescued from Mount Hood National Forest
video:video.video:description: An Oregon Army National Guard helicopter safely hoisted an injured logger from Mount Hood National Forest, after steep, rugged terrain prevented other means of rescue.

video:video.video:title: FBI says the man who attacked a Michigan synagogue died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound
video:video.video:description: Authorities say 41-year-old Ayman Mohamad Ghazali waited in his car outside a Detroit-area synagogue for two hours before ramming into the building.

video:video.video:title: Germany's Merz, Canada's Carney disagree with US' 30-day waiver on Russian oil sanctions
video:video.video:description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Friday that all other G7 countries disagreed with a U.S. move to ease sanctions on Russian oil.

video:video.video:title: US eases some Russian oil sanctions but crude prices stay high
video:video.video:description: The U.S. is temporarily easing some sanctions on Russian oil shipments, reflecting global concerns over sharply higher crude prices due to supply shortages stemming from the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Iranian missile hits Holon in central Israel with no reported casualties, AP explains
video:video.video:description: An Iranian missile struck a site in central Israel Friday evening. There were no casualties recorded as a result of the attacks throughout the day, according to authorities.

video:video.video:title: AP Reporter at site of Iranian strike in Central Israel
video:video.video:description: AP Reporter Sam Mednick was in central Israel at a site where an Iranian missile struck Friday. This came as Israel and the US continued their attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Rescue workers remove rubble after a building in Isfahan is hit by US-Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television broadcast footage Friday on its website showing damage to buildings and cars following a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Indigenous women tour Ecuador oil field as warning against Amazon drilling expansion
video:video.video:description: About 30 Indigenous women from seven Amazon nationalities traveled to Ecuador’s northern oil region to witness the environmental impacts of decades of oil and gas extraction, part of a workshop aimed at preparing communities for possible expansion of drilling.

video:video.video:title: Smoke, fire seen coming from US embassy compound in Baghdad after reported missile strike
video:video.video:description: A missile struck a helipad inside the U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad, two Iraqi security officials said.

video:video.video:title: ‘The Secret Agent’ director on Oscars in wartime
video:video.video:description: "The Secret Agent" writer-director Kleber Mendonça Filho says it's a balancing act for filmmakers to celebrate at the Oscars during a war in the Middle East. (March 13)

video:video.video:title: Could 'KPop Demon Hunters' sequel come 'sooner than we think'?
video:video.video:description: "KPop Demon Hunters" star Arden Cho says the newly-announced sequel may be arriving "sooner than we think." The animated Netflix hit is nominated for an Oscar.

video:video.video:title: Scream clubs: A vocal release for stress
video:video.video:description: Can you scream your way to happiness? Maybe not, but the scream clubs popping up around the U.S. promise some relief. (AP Video: Emilie Megnien, Laura Bargfeld)

video:video.video:title: Oscar-nominated Tunisian filmmaker: 'Cinema can change things'
video:video.video:description: Kaouther Ben Hania says "cinema can change things" as U.S. lawmakers back legislation calling for an investigation into the killing of the Palestinian girl at the heart of her Oscar-nominated film, "The Voice of Hind Rajab."

video:video.video:title: Oscar nominee Kate Hudson on family: 'We love being weird actors'
video:video.video:description: Kate Hudson says she's been celebrating her Oscar nomination for "Song Sung Blue" with her parents Kurt Russell and Goldie Hawn: "We love being weird actors." (March 13)

video:video.video:title: Lebanon's largest sports stadium turned into shelter for people displaced by conflict - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: In just 10 days, more than 800,000 people in Lebanon have been displaced by war, just over a year since the last conflict uprooted over a million from their homes.

video:video.video:title: Indiana man struggles to get a job as labor market remains under strain
video:video.video:description: Since losing his job last November, 25-year-old John Sand said he has applied to nearly 300 jobs, from social media roles to Amazon warehouses, but hasn’t found work yet.

video:video.video:title: Two long-lost episodes of 'Doctor Who' found
video:video.video:description: A film charity announced Friday that it has found two previously lost 1960s episodes of iconic sci-fi TV series "Doctor Who" among the possessions of a deceased collector.

video:video.video:title: Controlled explosions carried out to prevent ice jams on Russian rivers and reduce spring floods
video:video.video:description: A military engineering team of the Novosibirsk missile formation carried out controlled explosions Friday on a tributary of the Ob River in Russia's Siberia to prevent the formation of ice jams and reduce the risk of spring flooding.

video:video.video:title: Come with me to the Governors Ball preview
video:video.video:description: The Governors Ball is where Oscar winners and nominees will party after the ceremony, and AP got a sneak peek. Follow Sarah Jones-Smith inside.

video:video.video:title: Ready set roll! Conan O'Brien unveils Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: Oscars host Conan O'Brien rolls out the red carpet ahead of Sunday's event. The ceremony airs at 7 p.m. ET on ABC and will stream on Hulu for the first time.

video:video.video:title: Cuban president says talks were recently held with the US to resolve differences
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel said Friday that his government has held recent talks with the U.S., marking the first time the Caribbean country confirms such speculation.

video:video.video:title: An inside look at the Oscars Rolex Greenroom
video:video.video:description: Before and after hitting the Academy Awards stage, Hollywood's biggest stars can be found chatting backstage in the Oscars Greenroom hosted by watch maker Rolex.

video:video.video:title: Workers prepare Oscars red carpet ahead of Sunday ceremony
video:video.video:description: Workers are putting the finishing touches on the Oscars red carpet ahead of Sunday's Academy Awards ceremony in Hollywood, to be hosted by Conan O'Brien. (March 13)

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan on portraying twins in 'Sinners'
video:video.video:description: Journey to the Oscars: At the "Sinners" premiere last year, Michael B. Jordan explained the preparation that went into portraying his twin characters, Smoke and Stack.

video:video.video:title: Friend of ROTC leader killed in Old Dominion University shooting visits campus to honor him
video:video.video:description: Eddie Flack expressed his respect and appreciation for the men and women who serve in the military to protect our country, “It's just unfortunate that they have to fight a two-front war, domestic and abroad.”

video:video.video:title: 'One Battle After Another' stars on film's impact
video:video.video:description: Journey to the Oscars: Regina Hall, Teyana Taylor and Chase Infiniti say they wanted to start conversations with “One Battle After Another.”

video:video.video:title: Paralympians ski in shorts and T-shirts, and some ask: Shouldn’t these Games be earlier?
video:video.video:description: At the Paralympics, some athletes competed in shorts and T-shirts. The warmth is increasingly commonplace in March as temperatures rise.

video:video.video:title: Quds Day rallies take place in Tehran, Isfahan and Mashhad in Iran
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television aired footage showing thousands of people across the Islamic Republic taking part in the annual Quds Day.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana's alligator farms mix capitalism with conservation
video:video.video:description: Advocates say commercial alligator farming has helped preserve the species. Not all conservationists think that's a good thing, but for the farmers and luxury brands seeking to market their products as sustainable, it's made sense to tie conservation to capitalism.

video:video.video:title: Large explosion in Iran's capital, state TV reports
video:video.video:description: A large explosion struck Iran's capital, Tehran, on Friday Iranian state television reported. The explosion hit Ferdowsi Square, just down Enghelab Street from Tehran University, the epicenter of ongoing Quds, or Jerusalem Day demonstrations.

video:video.video:title: In Louisiana, alligator farms mix capitalism and conservation
video:video.video:description: A Louisiana farm that raises alligators for skins used in luxury goods also helps conserve the species.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says Iran's new supreme leader is 'disfigured' but didn't provide details
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is wounded and 'likely disfigured.'

video:video.video:title: Refusing to answer questions on Iran school strike, Hegseth says officer picked to lead investigation
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says U.S. Central Command has picked a lead officer to investigate a deadly strike on a school in Iran.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 13th: Hegseth says Iran's new leader is wounded; Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut suburb; Record number of political parties register for Haiti's election; Russian crews set off explosives to break up ice jams

video:video.video:title: Workers search rubble of damaged buildings after at least 8 people killed in Israeli strike in Sidon
video:video.video:description: At least eight people have been killed in an Israeli strike in the southern coastal city of Sidon, Lebanon’s Health Ministry said Friday. Nine others were wounded.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler 'not thinking about' potential Oscars history
video:video.video:description: Ryan Coogler could become the first Black filmmaker to win best director in the Academy Awards’ nearly century-long history, but says he's focused on the 'Sinners' team before Sunday's ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Cuban president confirms talks with US government to find solutions to problems given their severity and impact
video:video.video:description: In what some are calling a historic moment for Cuba-U.S. relations, and after days of intense speculation, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has confirmed that talks between Cuba and the United States have taken place.

video:video.video:title: How higher oil prices can affect your wallet
video:video.video:description: Oil prices are rising which makes the costs of gasoline, diesel and jet fuel more expensive. But you don’t have to drive a car to be affected

video:video.video:title: US forecasts blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome and atmospheric river all at once
video:video.video:description: The United States is getting slammed by a stretch of March weather extremes, from flooding rain to record heat and late-season snow.

video:video.video:title: How warm weather affects racing at the Winter Paralympics
video:video.video:description: At the Winter Paralympics, some athletes competed in shorts and T-shirts. The warmth is increasingly commonplace in March as temperatures rise with emissions.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 13th: All six crew of US refueling plane are dead; Blast rocks Tehran after Israel threatened to target area of rally; Cuban President Díaz-Canel confirms talks with US; Markwayne Mullin faces tough DHS confirmation.

video:video.video:title: Neighbor of man who rammed into Michigan synagogue reacts to attack
video:video.video:description: A neighbor of Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, who was killed by security after ramming a vehicle into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township near Detroit on Thursday, reacts to attack.

video:video.video:title: Democrats warn new Trump tariffs could cost families over $2,500 this year
video:video.video:description: President Trump is working to replace lost federal revenue after the Supreme Court struck down his tariffs.

video:video.video:title: Nobel laureate Machado rallies with Venezuelan diaspora in Chile, says there is a plan to return
video:video.video:description: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado on Thursday said millions who left their country can return and help rebuild it. (AP video shot by Mauricio Cuevas)

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of US strikes on Kharg Island, Iran
video:video.video:description: Video from the U.S. Central Command showed U.S. forces destroying targets on the ground, which it claims were in Kharg Island, Iran.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds at least 15, with peace talks stalled
video:video.video:description: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds at least 15, with peace talks stalled

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler 'not thinking about' potential Oscars history
video:video.video:description: Ryan Coogler shares what he is focused on heading into Oscar Sunday. His film "Sinners" has an historic 16 nominations.

video:video.video:title: 'KPop Demon Hunters' star "thrilled" for sequel
video:video.video:description: "KPop Demon Hunters" star Arden Cho hints that the newly-announced sequel to the Oscar-nominated animated feature may arrive sooner than we think.

video:video.video:title: Brandi Carlile on what Americana means to her
video:video.video:description: Brandi Carlile shares how she defines Americana. The singer-songwriter announced the expansion of “The Human Tour" Friday.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out after drone interception in UAE
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Saturday in Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates following an interception of a drone, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Iraq is caught in the crossfire of the Iran war, with attacks by both sides on its soil - AP explains
video:video.video:description: Iraq is getting caught in the crossfire of the Iran war as the only country facing strikes from both sides, and that threatens to drag the nation that has so far avoided two years of regional turmoil into a full-blown crisis.

video:video.video:title: AP reporters add context to Trump's reasons for attacking Iran
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration has been trying to explain why it struck Iran and killed its supreme leader, and the shifting reasons range from Iran’s nuclear program to its ballistic missiles capabilities.

video:video.video:title: Dust storm blows over Gaza as people are urged to protect themselves
video:video.video:description: First responders told Palestinians to stay home, especially those with respiratory illness, as a cloud of orange-tinted, thick dust spread across the enclave.

video:video.video:title: Indigenous women tour Ecuador oil field as warning against Amazon drilling expansion
video:video.video:description: About 30 Indigenous women from seven Amazon nationalities traveled to Ecuador’s northern oil region to witness the environmental impacts of decades of oil and gas extraction, part of a workshop aimed at preparing communities for possible expansion of drilling.

video:video.video:title: Homes damaged by falling debris after Pakistan says it intercepted drones launched by Taliban
video:video.video:description: Drone attacks on Pakistan intercepted by the country's military on Friday injured two children in the city of Quetta and damaged homes.

video:video.video:title: Iran warns UAE about possible attack on U.S military 'hideouts', urges citizens to evacuate
video:video.video:description: Iran's joint military command threatened to attack cities in the UAE, home to Dubai and one of the world's busiest airports, saying the U.S. used “ports, docks and hideouts” there to launch strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, without providing evidence.

video:video.video:title: Trump departs Trump International Golf Club in West Palm Beach
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump departs his golf club in West Palm Beach, as he spends this weekend at Mar-a-Lago.

video:video.video:title: Chicago River turns green for St. Patrick's day
video:video.video:description: The Chicago River was glowing a bright emerald green on Saturday as the city kicks off its St. Patrick’s Day celebrations, a tradition started in 1962 by the Chicago Journeymen Plumbers Local 130.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, March 13th: U.S. embassy struck by missile in Baghdad; U.S. hits military sites on Iran's Kharg Island; American flag raised at U.S. embassy in Venezuela; Chicago River turns green for St.

video:video.video:title: Comic connoisseurs and cosplayers gather in Prague for sixth annual Comic Con
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans gathered at the sixth annual Comic-Con Prague, a three-day pop culture event with interactive workshops, a gaming zone, multiple lectures, and insights into upcoming films.

video:video.video:title: US raises flag at embassy in Caracas after seven-year diplomatic freeze ends
video:video.video:description: The United States flag was raised again at the U.S. Embassy in Caracas on Saturday, seven years after it was lowered when Washington and Caracas cut diplomatic relations in 2019.

video:video.video:title: Police fire tear gas during protest against labor reform in Ecuador
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of workers, students, and teachers protested Friday in Ecuador’s capital Quito after the government extended daily work shift from eight to 10 hours, with a weekly cap of 40 hours.

video:video.video:title: AP Exclusive: Turkey's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan talks diplomatic efforts as regional war rages
video:video.video:description: Turkey’s foreign minister Hakan Fidan said Saturday that there is no serious initiative to resume negotiations between the United States and Iran but that he believes Iran is open to back-channel talks.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds cause property damage in the Detroit metropolitan area
video:video.video:description: Strong winds cause property damage in the Detroit metropolitan area

video:video.video:title: South Korea and US hold joint exercises as part of annual military drills
video:video.video:description: The South Korean and U.S. militaries on Saturday held a joint training exercise at a river in Yeoncheon, north of South Korea's capital, as part of their annual Freedom Shield combined exercise.

video:video.video:title: Villagers live in fear after drones attack nearby refinery in Irbil, Iraq
video:video.video:description: Locals in a village in Irbil were living in fear on Friday, after a drone strike hit the nearby Lanaz refinery in the Iraqi city.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim observes test of rocket launch systems with his daughter
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, accompanied by his teenage daughter, observed a live-fire test of multiple rocket launch systems, state media reported Sunday, a likely response to ongoing U.S.

video:video.video:title: Atlanta celebrates St. Patrick's day with a parade
video:video.video:description: Over 2,000 people participated in a St. Patrick’s Day parade in Atlanta on Saturday. The parade was part of boisterous celebrations being held in cities across the US.

video:video.video:title: 'Black rain' creates health, environmental risks as toxic smoke billows from bombed Iran oil depots
video:video.video:description: Airstrikes on Iran's oil depots are sending up billowing clouds of toxic smoke that health and environmental officials warn pose serious health threats to anyone who breathes or comes into contact with it.
